summary: Reauthenticated every declared source and both authorized test files, then passed the final focused style and nine-test verification gates without finding a production defect.

artifacts.name: run-session-verification.md
artifacts.content:
# Run Session Verification

## Identity

- Compilation: `sha256:b719fd06f811091968c14ed8ff531ed5cd9df22d90d050e76c355dd542a1aed6`
- Blueprint: `sha256:6c9dadb14ab7c5c3cf7ae1db73178027e912d929b39a8243cbca13fd18ba0cb8`
- Approved package: `sha256:56117917b1f230336e4a08c92283a785d488b9dd77e4fe32c9e0f261f5e5c5fa`

## Authentication

All 20 declared source bindings matched their exact raw SHA-256 digest and byte count before the final gates.

- `test/specialist-run-schema.test.mjs`: 12,562 bytes, `sha256:433a3257d077df66f842fc3928b06520c74cab7c861a0415881df91f5d226f7a`
- `test/specialist-run.test.mjs`: 32,721 bytes, `sha256:46b886df83f4096339d557fcaf0bab4db31f25097a1048df7136aaf93d51b0ef`
- Both authorized files are strict UTF-8 with normalized LF and contain no CR or TAB characters.

## Coverage

The suites independently cover strict closed schemas, exact constants and routes, public exports, packed-consumer compatibility bindings, root, chain, diamond, fan-out, fan-in, disconnected, and complete DAGs, differential V11 assessment, valid arrival permutations, fresh-process restore and inspection, expectation and substitution attacks, malformed UTF-8, duplicate keys, unknown fields, secrets, controls, lone surrogates, proxies, accessors, stale and conflicting handoffs, replay, transition precedence, every non-pass route, deep freezing, caller detachment, exact 1 MiB handoff acceptance, canonical base64 expansion, and one-unit-over rejection.

## Final Gates

- `npm.cmd exec --offline -- biome check test\specialist-run-schema.test.mjs test\specialist-run.test.mjs`: PASS, two files checked, no fixes applied.
- `node --test test\specialist-run-schema.test.mjs test\specialist-run.test.mjs`: PASS, 9 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped, 0 cancelled.

## Preserved Attempts

The first boundary fixture incorrectly placed the aggregate 1 MiB budget into one artifact and was correctly rejected by the existing 16 KiB text limit. The fixture was corrected to distribute the exact byte budget across 65 contract-declared artifacts; no production code changed. Initial style findings were corrected. Native `apply_patch` later failed before mutation under the known Windows sandbox, so the contract-authorized SHA-256-precondition-guarded PowerShell fallback was used and resulting bytes were immediately verified.

## Result

The V12 run-session schema, lifecycle, inspection, dependency, routing, restart, authority-boundary, public-export, and compatibility behaviors passed focused independent verification. No production defect was found.

assumptions: Focused tests imported the existing built package through its public self-reference; rebuilding distribution artifacts was outside the exact two-file write authority.
risks: The full canonical npm verify and packed-consumer packaging gates were not run because they can write outside the declared scope.
followUps: The integration owner should rebuild the release candidate and run the canonical repository and packed-consumer gates before approval.
